brief_notes: Strong computational biology paper presenting DeepTernary for ternary complex prediction with clear state-of-the-art improvements (DockQ 0.65 vs 0.42 for AlphaFold3) and practical speed advantages (7s vs 5min). The BSA-degradation potency correlation adds functional relevance. Well-structured with comprehensive benchmarks against multiple baselines. Weaknesses include reliance on binary input structures as acknowledged, relatively small BSA validation set (40 targets), and no ablation study of architectural components (intra-graph vs inter-graph attention contributions).
